OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS (1) VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., (2) FRANK NOLAN AND (3) ANDREW WARREN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 72569902) FILED BY PLAINTIFF TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY CORPORATION

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION, 501, DEFENDANT FRANK NOLAN, ANDREW WARREN, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE SMITH DECLARATION FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED "IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" AS REQUIRED BY CCP 430.41. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE. THE RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY MAY 3, 2024. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE DEMURRER AS FILED APPEARS TO BE RUSHED. FOR EXAMPLE, COMPARE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER. IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES MOVING PARTY STATES THAT "THE DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED AGAINST DEFENDANTS SHOULD BE SUSTAINED AND DEFENDANTS FRANK NOLAN AND ANDREW WARREN SHOULD BE DISMISSED." (MOVING 5:7-9). THIS RELIEF AS TO THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS NOT MENTIONED IN THE NOTICE OF DEMURRER, DEMURRER AND NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY ARGUMENT OR AUTHORITIES IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. ADDITIONALLY, HAD DEFENDANTS PROVIDED AUTHORITIES TO THE PLAINTIFF IN GOOD FAITH MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS, PLAINTIFF MIGHT HAVE AMENDED THE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS DID NOT DO SO DURING WRITTEN EXCHANGE IN ADVANCE OF PROPER "MEET AND CONFER" AND APPEAR TO NOT HAVE DONE SO IN THE DEMURRER EITHER. SEE MISJOINDER DISCUSSION (MOVING 6:19-23). IF DEFENDANT RAISES THIS ISSUE IN A SUBSEQUENT RESPONSE, DEFENDANT TO CITE AUTHORITIES ON POINT THAT ACTUALLY DISCUSS EMPLOYEE LIABILITY AND PROPRIETY OF NAMING EMPLOYEES AS DEFENDANTS; DISMISSAL OF EMPLOYEES IN CASES WHERE VICARIOUS LIABILITY IS ALLEGED/PROVEN AS TO THE EMPLOYER, ETC. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MAY-15-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS (1) VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., (2) ANDREW WARREN, AND (3) FRANK NOLAN'S COMPLIANCE WITH COURT ORDER AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT ORDERS THE VANGUARD DEFENDANTS (ANDREW WARREN, FRANK NOLAN, AND VANGUARD PROPERTIES INC.) TO PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF TOWERVIEW SF II LLC'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER THAT EXCLUDE ANY OBJECTIONS BASED ON JOINT DEFENSE PRIVILEGE, LACK OF NOTICE TO CONSUMERS, OR EQUAL AVAILABILITY OF DOCUMENTS TO THE PLAINTIFF, BY MAY 22, 2025 (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan'S Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOHN O'CONNOR, Esq. of Clark Hill LLP (tel: 415-984-8500) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. MICHAEL SMITH, Esq. of Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP (tel: 415-217-6990) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC's Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan'S Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court orders the Vanguard Defendants (Andrew Warren, Frank Nolan, and Vanguard Properties Inc.) to provide verified responses to plaintiff Towerview SF II LLC's Requests for Production of Documents within 10 days of entry of this order that exclude any objections based on joint defense privilege, lack of notice to consumers, or equal availability of documents to the plaintiff, by May 22, 2025. The Vanguard Defendants are ordered to provide a privilege log and all responsive documents by May 29, 2025. The Vanguard Defendants' failure to comply with this court's discovery order lacks substantial justification and subsequent noncompliance may subject these defendants to evidentiary or issue sanctions. The court orders the defendants to pay $5000 to Towerview SF II, LLC in sanctions by May 29, 2025. The court does not award the full sanctions amount requested because Towerview did not obtain all the relief it sought and because Towerview did not break out its counsel's time between time spent on the motion (necessary to enforce discovery obligations) and time spent meeting and confer (an anticipated aspect of the discovery process). Each of the Vanguard Defendants is jointly and severally liable for the sanctions payment.

Counsel for the plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan To Further Respond To Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Mar-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOHN O'CONNOR, Esq. of Clark Hill LLP (tel: 415-984-8500) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. MICHAEL SMITH, Esq. of Manning & Kass Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP (tel: 415-217-6990) for Defendants Vanguard Properties, Inc., Frank Nolan and Andrew Warren.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC's Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan To Further Respond To Requests For Production Of Documents.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Towerview SF II LLC's motion to compel is granted in part and denied in part. Towerview has not complied with CRC, Rule 3.1345(a), which requires a separate statement. Towerview contends the court should exercise its discretion to excuse the lack, but a separate statement serves important purposes for the court and it is unwieldy and time-consuming for the court to fully address a motion like this without one. That said, the Vanguard defendants have agreed in their opposition to "serve verified responses from which the Joint Defense Privilege, Notice to Consumer deficiency and Equally Available to Plaintiff objections are removed, and which confirm only documents covered by the attorney client privilege or attorney work product privilege are withheld from production [and] to produce a copy of the applicable insurance policy, redacted for premium information." (Opp. 4:7-13.) The Vanguard defendants are ordered to do so by April 10, 2025 and to provide a privilege log for any documents withheld pursuant to any privilege by the same date. The court finds that both parties' positions in this motion lacked substantial justification, and it does not award discovery sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

***** Minute order amended on 8/13/2025 to reflect the following disclosure: Approximately 10 years ago, Judge Van Aken and her husband were represented by defendant Andrew Warren in making a purchase offer on a property. The purchase did not go through. Warren showed Judge Van Aken one or two other properties in the intervening years but not more recently than five years ago. Judge Van Aken has had not had contact with Warren in at least the last five years. These circumstances do not give rise to disqualification (see CCP 170.1) but the court nonetheless discloses them. *****

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Timothy M. Flaherty; Clark Hill LLP; 415-984-8500; appearing as counsel for Plaintiff Towerview SF II, LLC.
Michael L. Smith; Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP; 415-217-6990; appearing as counsel for defendants Vanguard Properties, Inc, Frank Nolan and Andrew Warren.

Not contested. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to identify the general damages sought as to each cause of action asserted against the moving defendants. Complaint is silent on this issue and the opposition only states what is not being sought (emotional distress).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Timothy M. Flaherty; Clark Hill LLP; 415-984-8500; appearing as counsel for Plaintiff Towerview SF II, LLC.
Michael L. Smith; Manning & Kass, Ellrod, Ramirez, Trester LLP; 415-217-6990; appearing as counsel for defendants Vanguard Properties, Inc, Frank Nolan and Andrew Warren.

Defendants contest the court's tentative decision. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Causes of action for negligence and negligent misrepresentation are sufficiently stated. Request for judicial notice of the Agents Visual Inspection Report is denied.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT FRANK NOLAN, ANDREW WARREN, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The Smith Declaration fails to demonstrate that the parties met and conferred "in person or by telephone" as required by CCP 430.41.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT FRANK NOLAN, ANDREW WARREN, VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The Smith Declaration fails to demonstrate that the parties met and conferred "in person or by telephone" as required by CCP 430.41. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the Complaint is now due by May 3, 2024. The Court notes that the demurrer as filed appears to be rushed. For example, compare Points and Authorities, Notice of Demurrer and Demurrer. In the points and authorities moving party states that "the demurrer to the fourth and fifth causes of action alleged against Defendants should be sustained and Defendants FRANK NOLAN and ANDREW WARREN should be dismissed." (Moving 5:7-9). This relief as to the 4th and 5th causes of action is not mentioned in the Notice of Demurrer, Demurrer and not supported by any argument or authorities in the Points and Authorities. Additionally, had Defendants provided authorities to the Plaintiff in good faith meet and confer efforts, Plaintiff might have amended the complaint. Defendants did not do so during written exchange in advance of proper "meet and confer" and appear to not have done so in the demurrer either. See misjoinder discussion (Moving 6:19-23). If Defendant raises this issue in a subsequent response, Defendant to cite authorities on point that actually discuss employee liability and propriety of naming employees as defendants; dismissal of employees in cases where vicarious liability is alleged/proven as to the employer, etc.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
